_meta.purpose: Machine-readable term map consumed by Tier-1 TargetAdapter and Tier-2 templates. Keys are source-framework concepts; values are the authoritative MAF equivalents.

StateGraph.notes: A LangGraph StateGraph with explicit nodes and edges maps to a MAF Workflow. Do NOT flatten into a linear run() function.

CompiledGraph.notes: The compiled artifact is the same Workflow object returned by WorkflowBuilder.build().

ReactAgent.notes: A single LLM agent with tool-calling in a loop maps to ChatAgent.

TypedDict.notes: Graph-level TypedDict state is replaced by typed message models passed along edges. For cross-cutting values use WorkflowContext shared state.

Annotated[list, add].notes: Drop Annotated and operator.add entirely. Carry the list in the message model and append directly.

shared_state.notes: Use for values that many executors need to read or write (audit_log, project_id, etc.).

node_function.imports:
from agent_framework import Executor, handler, WorkflowContext
# or
from agent_framework import executor, WorkflowContext
node_function.return_convention: Call await ctx.send_message(TypedOutput(...)) instead of returning a dict. Never return a bare partial dict.

final_node.notes: A terminal node that produces the workflow output uses WorkflowContext[None, OutputType] and calls await ctx.yield_output(...) instead of send_message.

add_edge.notes: Direct unconditional edge. Executors are passed by reference, not string name.

add_conditional_edges.maf_api: One .add_edge(src, target, condition=lambda m: ...) per branch
add_conditional_edges.notes: The router function becomes a predicate on the emitted message. Every branch needs its own edge call.
add_conditional_edges.pattern:
.add_edge(validate, approve,  condition=lambda m: m.passed)
.add_edge(validate, retry,   condition=lambda m: not m.passed and m.retries < MAX)
.add_edge(validate, drop,    condition=lambda m: not m.passed and m.retries >= MAX)

cycle_loop.source_pattern: graph with a cycle (node A -> node B -> node A)
cycle_loop.maf_pattern: back-edge guarded by a termination condition
cycle_loop.pattern:
.add_edge(gap_gen, validation)
.add_edge(validation, gap_gen,  condition=lambda m: not m.passed and m.retries < MAX)
.add_edge(validation, drop,     condition=lambda m: not m.passed and m.retries >= MAX)
.add_edge(validation, next_step,condition=lambda m: m.passed)
cycle_loop.notes: Always include an iteration-cap condition to guarantee termination.

fan_in.notes: Waits for all branches before proceeding to the join executor.

@tool.maf_equivalent: plain typed function, or @ai_function to override name/description
@tool.registration: Pass the function in tools=[...] to ChatAgent, or call directly inside an executor.
@tool.anti_pattern: Wrapper classes decorated with @kernel_function that are never instantiated or registered.
@tool.notes: Do not emit dead tool-wrapper classes. Either wire the tool in properly or call the function directly.

agent_as_tool.notes: Use to compose agents — pass a sub-agent as a tool to a supervisor agent.

interrupt.import: from agent_framework import RequestInfoExecutor, RequestInfoMessage, RequestResponse
interrupt.notes: Always keep an automated fast-path. Never implement HITL as commented-out code with a hard-coded auto-approve as the only live path.

providers.notes: MAF is provider-pluggable. The chat client is injected; swap the import to change provider.
providers.gemini_note: If the source used Gemini, use an OpenAI-compatible Gemini endpoint with OpenAIChatClient, or swap to AzureOpenAIChatClient. The MAF framework itself does not change.

reject_list.description: Any generated output containing the following is a conversion error.
items: linear run(ctx) calling nodes in sequence when source had conditional edges
router function defined but not wired into .add_edge condition
tool wrapper class never instantiated or registered
HITL as auto-approve-only with real logic in comments
stdlib module in requirements.txt (zipfile, json, io, os, sys, re, math, hashlib)
